title: SuperBEST v4 Node Costs — Economics Equations

cross_domain_isomorphisms.equation: power law scaling P = C * x^a * y^b
cross_domain_isomorphisms.note: Any bivariate power law has this 10n structure

breakdown_add_gen.note: required when rho < 0, powers can be negative

special_cases.breakdown: sub(1,alpha)=2 + mul(alpha,x)=2 + mul(1malpha,y)=2 + add_pos=3 - 2 (share sub) => 2+2+2+2+mul(sub,y)=2 => sub=2, mul=2, mul=2, add_pos=3 = 9 ... simplified: sub(1,alpha)=2 + 2 muls=4 + add_pos=3 => but we already have sub, so 2+2+2+3=9... see note
special_cases.note: sub(1,alpha)=2n, mul(alpha,x)=2n, mul(1malpha,y)=2n, add_pos=3n => 9n total. Correction from 7n: 9n.

special_cases.breakdown: pow(x,alpha)=3 + sub(1,alpha)=2 + pow(y,1malpha)=3 + mul=2 => 10n. Same as Cobb-Douglas production.

special_cases.note: min() is not differentiable, not representable as EML expression

equations.name: Log Utility
equations.significance: Cheapest utility function. Log utility is a single EML primitive node. Widely used in macro (CRRA limit as gamma->1), finance (Kelly criterion), information theory (entropy).

cross_domain_isomorphisms.note: Shannon entropy uses ln as primitive; per-term cost is ln=1 + mul=2 + neg=2 = 5n before summation
Boltzmann entropy is also a single ln node (k_B is constant)

equations.name: CRRA Utility (Constant Relative Risk Aversion)

breakdown.note: result can be negative if gamma > 1

equations.limit_note: As gamma -> 1, CRRA -> log utility (1n). The limit is 7x cheaper in node cost.

cross_domain_isomorphisms.equation: power law potential V(r) = V_0 * r^n / n
cross_domain_isomorphisms.note: Same structure: pow(r,n)=3 + div by n=2 + mul V_0=2 = 7n

equations.name: Price Elasticity of Demand

breakdown_point.note: dQ/dP from derivative, not counted here

equations.sign_note: epsilon < 0 for normal goods; result is signed so no add_gen issue here (pure division chain).

revenue_maximizing_rate.note: t* = 0.5 is a constant, zero computational cost

cross_domain_isomorphisms.equation: P = I * (EMF - I*R) = EMF*I - R*I^2 (power in circuit with back-EMF)
cross_domain_isomorphisms.note: Parabolic in I; same t*(1-t) structure after normalization

discounting_factor_building_block.significance: Core building block of all time-value-of-money calculations

cross_domain_isomorphisms.equation: bond price P = sum coupon/(1+r)^t + face/(1+r)^N
cross_domain_isomorphisms.note: Nearly identical to DCF with positive CFs (12N-3); extra terms for face value

equations.name: Gini Coefficient (Discrete)
equations.note: Linear algebra operations not counted; formula assumes sorted incomes y_1 <= y_2 <= ... <= y_N

equations.name: Net Present Value (NPV)
equations.note: NPV = DCF + one subtraction. C_0 > 0 (initial investment), PV_sum sign depends on cash flows.

special_case_continuous.note: Continuous compounding collapses 12n -> 5n. Same as population growth P*exp(r*t).

cross_domain_isomorphisms.note: Continuous compounding = exponential population growth, identical 5n structure
neg + mul + exp = 2+2+1=5n. Same as continuous compounding with negative rate.

key_observations.observation: Log utility is the cheapest utility function at 1n — it is an EML primitive
key_observations.detail: U = ln(C) costs 1n. CRRA costs 7n. CES costs 19-27n. The macroeconomic preference for log utility (Ramsey model, Kelly criterion) has a direct cost-complexity justification.
Y = A*L^alpha*K^beta and rate = k*[A]^m*[B]^n are mathematically identical. Different science, same EML structure. Isomorphism class: coefficient * power1 * power2.

key_observations.observation: DCF with positive CFs (12N-3) ≈ Bond pricing (12N+6)
key_observations.detail: Bond pricing adds face value term but shares the same per-period discounting structure. The discounting factor 1/(1+r)^t costs exactly 7n and is the universal time-value building block.
Discrete compound interest A = P*(1+r/n)^(nt) costs 12n. The continuous limit A = P*exp(rt) costs 5n — a 2.4x complexity reduction. Shared with population growth and radioactive decay.

key_observations.observation: CRRA limit: as gamma->1, cost drops from 7n to 1n
key_observations.detail: CRRA utility with gamma=1 is exactly log utility. The limit is 7x cheaper. This is the deepest cost discontinuity in standard utility functions.

key_observations.observation: CES special cases span 9n-27n depending on rho
key_observations.detail: rho->1 (perfect substitutes): 9n. rho->0 (Cobb-Douglas): 10n. General CES: 19n (add_pos) or 27n (add_gen). The add_gen penalty (11n vs 3n) dominates when rho<0.

key_observations.observation: Discounting factor 1/(1+r)^t = 7n is the universal time-value building block
key_observations.detail: add_pos(1,r)=3 + pow=3 + recip=1 = 7n. Every DCF, NPV, bond price, annuity formula contains this subexpression.
